entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop susceptible to fungal invasion and mycotoxin contamination.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: A toxigenic fungus known to cause aflatoxin contamination in groundnuts.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where groundnut growers have not adopted recommended practices for contamination reduction.

entities.name: GENETIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherent ability of a plant to resist seed invasion by fungi and the production of aflatoxins.

entities.name: SEED INVASION
entities.description: The process by which fungi like Aspergillus flavus enter groundnut seeds.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN
entities.description: A potent mycotoxin produced by toxigenic strains of Aspergillus flavus.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins in groundnuts resulting from fungal invasion.

entities.name: FUNGI
entities.description: A group of organisms that can associate with groundnut seeds and produce mycotoxins.

entities.name: MYCOTOXINS
entities.description: Toxic secondary metabolites produced by fungi that can contaminate groundnuts.

entities.name: CITRININ
entities.description: A specific type of mycotoxin found naturally in groundnuts.

entities.name: ZEARALENONE
entities.description: A specific type of mycotoxin found naturally in groundnuts.
